title: Chrono Cross: The Radical Dreamers Edition (1133760)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: CHRONO CROSS: THE RADICAL DREAMERS EDITION
- Steam AppID of the game: 1133760

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 580
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 22.0.0
- Kernel version: 5.16.0-6-amd64 (Debian Testing)
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/megatog615/f3dcd17f943eb871016b1c422dec2286
- Proton version: Experimental as well as 7.0-1

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1133760.log](https://gist.github.com/megatog615/516aafd9aef5e84b2ad4dfec14cda042)

## Symptoms 
The game's launcher appears to use .NET, which seems to throw a tantrum. Continuing doesn't yield a usable result(black screen). I've linked a screenshot of the error box as well as the full text of the error below:
![Screenshot_20220407_145326](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3255620/162276414-94a21258-403a-459f-8c89-2d6c0733c11d.png)
[.NET error log](https://gist.github.com/megatog615/af16c74e1742b80d2933e3d6c39a896b)

## Reproduction
Launch the game from Steam.

metadata.issue_title: Chrono Cross: The Radical Dreamers Edition (1133760)

title: BeamNG.drive keyboard issues
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: `BeamNG.drive`
- Steam AppID of the game: `284160`

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6600
- Video driver version: `radv`, Mesa 25.0.0-1
- Kernel version: 6.12.16-amd64
- Link to full system information report: [Gist](https://gist.github.com/marshallovski/1ddf7291c93e3e8d50ce3cd339a14f81), [Gist: Steam Runtime Diagnostics](https://gist.github.com/marshallovski/117da2623652cccc4082efd1098cf189)
- Proton version: 9.0-4, Hotfix

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


[Proton logs](https://limewire.com/d/h5DsP#hVcXaC0DZm)


## Symptoms 
Keyboard keys are broken, wrong keys are being typed.
I have a problem: keyboard keys are broken. Before "0.35.0.0.18025" update it worked fine.
Example screenshot: http://cringe.rf.gd/uploads/20250402191104_1.jpg 
on this screenshot, I have set my keyboard layout to "us" and pressed "P" key. 
Gamepad controls are working fine, also all non-letter keys on keyboard also working fine (screenshot: http://cringe.rf.gd/uploads/20250402191506_1.jpg).

My machine environment: 
CPU: AMD Ryzen 5 5600X
GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6600
RAM: 64GB
OS: Debian trixie/sid x86_64
Kernel: Linux 6.12.16-amd64
System locale: uk_UA.UTF-8
Keyboard layout used in game: QWERTY, us (all other keyboard languages also didn't worked)

Tried with Proton 9.0-4 and Proton Hotfix, and the issue still persist.

## Reproduction
1. Open BeamNG.drive
2. Open Controls
3. Press "Hardware"
4. Press any button

title: Fluid Lander - フリュードランダー (1064950)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Fluid Lander - フリュードランダー
- Steam AppID of the game: 1064950

## System Information
- GPU: Intel HD Graphics 520
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.0.3
- Kernel version: 5.11.0-34-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/FurretUber/643ce83a840feddb4a41730de91ae2cc
- Proton version: 6.3-6 (tried with experimental too)

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.



[steam-1064950.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7190901/steam-1064950.log)

## Symptoms 

The game runs slow. I'm not sure if it's a problem or just that an i3-6100U can't really simulate CFD in real time. I tried lowering all settings, improving from 23 to 27 FPS. `htop` shows around 110%, `intel_gpu_top` shows 90% Render.

Other than that, from what I tested (defeated the first boss), the game was normal.

## Reproduction

1) Open the game;
2) Start a stage;
3) Notice low FPS.

metadata.issue_title: Fluid Lander - フリュードランダー (1064950)

title: Warpips (1291010)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Warpips
- Steam AppID of the game: 1291010

## System Information
- GPU: Intel Raptor Lake-P [Iris Xe Graphics]
- Video driver version: Mesa 22.3
- Kernel version: 6.1.0
- Link to full system information report: https://gist.github.com/jkirk/eb76f28d5400a6f05c1991b5a6c4b551
- Proton version: 9.0-4

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.



## Symptoms 

Game starts, but after "Loading" is shown FPS drops to 1.

Workaround: Properties > General > Launch Options: `PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%`

## Reproduction

* Just start the game

[steam-1291010.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21998519/steam-1291010.log)
